description: The Arundel site sits at the meeting point of historic Grassy Woodland and Valley Grassy Forest systems that once characterised this part of Surrey Hills, shaped by gentle slopes, well-drained soils, and open canopies of eucalypts. Over time, urban development has simplified these ecologies, fragmenting habitat, compacting soil, and reducing the diversity of ground and shrub layers that once supported rich insect and bird life. Despite this, the land still holds its ecological memory visible in the way water moves across the site, in remnant soil profiles, and in its proximity to South Surrey Park. This garden responds directly to that context, using layered planting, dense groundcover, and woodland structure to re-establish ecological function at a domestic scale. In doing so, Arundel becomes a small but meaningful act of repair, reconnecting the site to the broader landscape and contributing to the long-term resilience of local ecological systems.

activity_log.title: Pre-design site analysis -- ecological context mapping
activity_log.notes: Site analysis completed prior to design. Pre-1770 EVC mapping confirmed Grassy Woodland as the dominant vegetation community. Present-day context shows fragmented remnants -- Valley Grassy Forest in drainage lines, Wattle Park and Surrey Hills Reserve as nearest intact EVCs. Property boundary confirmed via VicPlan -- NRZ3 zone with PPRZ directly adjacent confirming corridor node potential.

activity_log.title: Site documentation -- pre-installation baseline
activity_log.notes: Comprehensive photographic site documentation. 19 photographs record pre-installation condition -- slope, existing brick walls and stone steps, weed pressure, rainwater tank infrastructure, plant staging, and direct park adjacency. Visual baseline established for ecological change tracking.

activity_log.title: Excavation, soil removal and weeding
activity_log.notes: Unwanted and contaminated soil excavated and removed from slope. Existing plants removed across planting zones. Weeding completed. Slope cleared to bare soil ready for contouring and amendment.

activity_log.title: Excavation, soil removal and weeding -- day 2
activity_log.notes: Second day of site preparation. Excavation and soil removal continued. Existing plants removed, weeding completed across remaining zones. Slope cleared and settled -- small tree planted on upper slope.

activity_log.title: Excavation, soil removal and weeding -- day 3
activity_log.notes: Third and final day of site preparation. Excavation and soil removal completed across all zones. Existing plants removed, weeding finalised. Logs staged on slope ready for habitat embedding. Site preparation complete.

activity_log.title: Excavation and soil removal -- Kanga loader deployed
activity_log.notes: Kanga mini loader deployed for excavation and soil removal. Contaminated soil and existing plants cleared across all remaining zones. Four days of preparation now complete.

activity_log.title: Excavation, soil removal and weeding -- day 5
activity_log.notes: Fifth day of preparation. Full aerial view of cleared slope from deck -- 475m2 visible in cleared state, Reserve canopy in spring flush beyond fence. Contaminated soil and plant material removal continuing.

activity_log.title: Design presented -- layered ecological planting system
activity_log.notes: Full ecological design presented to Andrew and Elizabeth. Five-layer planting system across 475m2 rear garden -- canopy (~32 trees), sub-canopy (~42 trees), shrub (~262 plants), ground layer (~714 plants), and swale/water garden. Form and element strategy documents waterway hydrology and habitat structure. All species drawn from Grassy Woodland EVC context confirmed in site analysis.

activity_log.title: Ecological baseline and preparation -- return visit
activity_log.notes: Return visit months after clearance. Six soil samples collected across site zones -- sandy, low organic matter, biologically active. Six weed species identified and hand-removed without chemicals: Flaxleaf fleabane, Common sow-thistle, Panic veldt grass, Barley grass, Wireweed, California poppy. Active ant colonies and soil insects observed. Site in settling and observation phase.

activity_log.title: Day 3 site report -- mechanical shaping and pathway definition
activity_log.notes: Kanga mini loader used for mechanical weed removal, pathway alignment, landform shaping, and surface consolidation. Intentionally shallow and controlled. Soil biology preserved. Site ready for sub-base introduction.

activity_log.title: Day 4 site report -- sub-base installation and path definition
activity_log.notes: Crushed rock sub-base partially installed across pathway zones. Path line visible from above for first time. Sub-base watered in. Granite rocks relocated for gabion reuse. Path curve follows design intent.

activity_log.title: Ground contouring and soil preparation
activity_log.notes: Compacted surface layers removed by hand scraping. Gentle contours shaped to direct water to planting zones. Existing self-seeded natives retained in situ.

activity_log.title: Habitat structure installed -- embedded logs and voids
activity_log.notes: Logs partially embedded along contour line 2 to retain moisture and create cool refuges for invertebrates. Three nodes mapped and recorded.

activity_log.title: Burrowing frog sighting -- lower slope refuge zone
activity_log.notes: Presence indicates moist subsurface refuge, functional soil biology, and low-toxicity conditions. Adds to corridor node weighting for this garden.

activity_log.title: Planting day -- 23 species installed, ponds, rocks, logs and mulch
activity_log.notes: First planting day. 23 species installed including two eucalypts (E. leucoxylon connata, E. radiata), Kunzea baxteri, Correa lawrenceana, Grevillea Red Rambler, Hardenbergia, Viola, Poa, Austrostipa, Dichelachne, Austrodanthonia and groundcovers. 6 ponds installed. Rocks and logs placed throughout. First layers of mulch and soil complete. Five structural layers now present.

notes: Values reflect installation stage. Canopy, layers and soil health will be reassessed at 6 and 12 months.
